heading: Definitions
label: 9501 Definitions
notes: References in TextThe Head Start Act, referred to in par. (17)(B), is subchapter B (§§ 635–657) of chapter 8 of subtitle A of title VI of , , , which is classified generally to subchapter II (§ 9831 et seq.) of chapter 105 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The terms “elementary school”, “secondary school”, “local educational agency”, and “State educational agency” have the meanings given those terms in .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: Outlying areas
children.label: B Outlying areas
children.text: The term “outlying areas” has the meaning given such term in .section 6331(c) of this title

children.heading: Freely associated states
children.label: C Freely associated states
children.text: The term “freely associated states” means the Republic of the Marshall Islands, the Federated States of Micronesia, and the Republic of Palau.

children.label: A
children.text: to gain knowledge or understanding necessary for determining the means by which a recognized and specific need may be met; and

children.label: B
children.text: that is specifically directed to the advancement of practice in the field of education.

children.label: A
children.text: to gain fundamental knowledge or understanding of phenomena and observable facts, without specific application toward processes or products; and

children.label: B
children.text: for the advancement of knowledge in the field of education.

children.heading: Board
children.label: 4 Board
children.text: The term “Board” means the National Board for Education Sciences established under .section 9516 of this title

children.heading: Bureau
children.label: 5 Bureau
children.text: The term “Bureau” means the Bureau of Indian Affairs.

children.heading: Comprehensive center
children.label: 6 Comprehensive center
children.text: The term “comprehensive center” means an entity established under .section 9602 of this title

children.heading: Department
children.label: 7 Department
children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Education.

children.heading: Development
children.label: 8 Development
children.text: The term “development” means the systematic use of knowledge or understanding gained from the findings of scientifically valid research and the shaping of that knowledge or understanding into products or processes that can be applied and evaluated and may prove useful in areas such as the preparation of materials and new methods of instruction and practices in teaching, that lead to the improvement of the academic skills of students, and that are replicable in different educational settings.

children.heading: Director
children.label: 9 Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of the Institute of Education Sciences.

children.heading: Dissemination
children.label: 10 Dissemination
children.text: The term “dissemination” means the communication and transfer of the results of scientifically valid research, statistics, and evaluations, in forms that are understandable, easily accessible, and usable, or adaptable for use in, the improvement of educational practice by teachers, administrators, librarians, other practitioners, researchers, parents, policymakers, and the public, through technical assistance, publications, electronic transfer, and other means.

children.heading: Early childhood educator
children.label: 11 Early childhood educator
children.text: The term “early childhood educator” means a person providing, or employed by a provider of, nonresidential child care services (including center-based, family-based, and in-home child care services) that is legally operating under State law, and that complies with applicable State and local requirements for the provision of child care services to children at any age from birth through the age at which a child may start kindergarten in that State.

children.heading: Field-initiated research
children.label: 12 Field-initiated research
children.text: The term “field-initiated research” means basic research or applied research in which specific questions and methods of study are generated by investigators (including teachers and other practitioners) and that conforms to standards of scientifically valid research.

children.heading: Historically Black college or university
children.label: 13 Historically Black college or university
children.text: The term “historically Black college or university” means a part B institution as defined in .section 1061 of this title

children.heading: Institute
children.label: 14 Institute
children.text: The term “Institute” means the Institute of Education Sciences established under .section 9511 of this title

children.heading: Institution of higher education
children.label: 15 Institution of higher education
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given that term in .section 1001(a) of this title

children.heading: National research and development center
children.label: 16 National research and development center
children.text: The term “national research and development center” means a research and development center supported under .section 9533(c) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: child care providers;

children.label: B
children.text: Head Start agencies operating Head Start programs, and entities carrying out Early Head Start programs, under the Head Start Act ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 9831

children.label: C
children.text: preschools;

children.label: D
children.text: kindergartens; and

children.label: E
children.text: libraries.

children.label: i
children.text: apply rigorous, systematic, and objective methodology to obtain reliable and valid knowledge relevant to education activities and programs; and

children.label: ii
children.text: present findings and make claims that are appropriate to and supported by the methods that have been employed.

children.label: i
children.text: employing systematic, empirical methods that draw on observation or experiment;

children.label: ii
children.text: involving data analyses that are adequate to support the general findings;

children.label: iii
children.text: relying on measurements or observational methods that provide reliable data;

children.label: iv
children.text: making claims of causal relationships only in random assignment experiments or other designs (to the extent such designs substantially eliminate plausible competing explanations for the obtained results);

children.label: v
children.text: ensuring that studies and methods are presented in sufficient detail and clarity to allow for replication or, at a minimum, to offer the opportunity to build systematically on the findings of the research;

children.label: vi
children.text: obtaining acceptance by a peer-reviewed journal or approval by a panel of independent experts through a comparably rigorous, objective, and scientific review; and

children.label: vii
children.text: using research designs and methods appropriate to the research question posed.

children.label: A
children.text: adheres to the highest possible standards of quality with respect to research design and statistical analysis;

children.label: B
children.text: provides an adequate description of the programs evaluated and, to the extent possible, examines the relationship between program implementation and program impacts;

children.label: C
children.text: provides an analysis of the results achieved by the program with respect to its projected effects;

children.label: D
children.text: employs experimental designs using random assignment, when feasible, and other research methodologies that allow for the strongest possible causal inferences when random assignment is not feasible; and

children.label: E
children.text: may study program implementation through a combination of scientifically valid and reliable methods.

children.heading: Scientifically valid research
children.label: 20 Scientifically valid research
children.text: The term “scientifically valid research” includes applied research, basic research, and field-initiated research in which the rationale, design, and interpretation are soundly developed in accordance with scientifically based research standards.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 21 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Education.

children.heading: State
children.label: 22 State
children.text: The term “State” includes (except as provided in ) each of the 50 States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the freely associated states, and the outlying areas.section 9548 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: improved educational and other practices and classroom instruction based on scientifically valid research; and

children.label: ii
children.text: improved planning, design, and administration of programs;

children.label: B
children.text: assistance in interpreting, analyzing, and utilizing statistics and evaluations; and

children.label: C
children.text: other assistance necessary to encourage the improvement of teaching and learning through the applications of techniques supported by scientifically valid research.
